title: Freeze audit corpus, authority order, matrix schema, and entity inventory
description: Establish the reproducible audit baseline: actual .specs/ and docs/ paths, local authority, entity inventory, prior audit provenance, evidence schema, and external corpus identities. Pin Matt Pocock Skills to /home/jmercier/Codes/third-parties/mattpocock_skills revision 9603c1cc8118d08bc1b3bf34cf714f62178dea3b, Spec Kit and GSD to their recorded revisions. acceptance_criteria: Baseline records 34 spec files, 52 doc files, 17 skills, 13 agents, and 12 top-level recipes or explains inventory changes; authority order is explicit; local normative material is distinguished from comparative Spec Kit, GSD, and Matt Pocock Skills at revision 9603c1cc8118d08bc1b3bf34cf714f62178dea3b; external guidance is not promoted without adoption evidence; finding key is concept + canonical contract + artifact set + root cause.
notes:
Delegation: produce the frozen evidence schema and corpus inventory. Read-only. Architect reviews authority ordering before closure.
Corpus addition: Goose comparative source (external, non-normative): /home/jmercier/Codes/third-parties/goose; origin https://github.com/aaif-goose/goose.git; inspected local branch feat/azure-foundry-provider at exact revision 2de5569b046061994942c16be60d8fcd8da25849 (2026-07-03). Cite exact revision + repository-relative path/line or symbol. Classify differences using the audit authority model; Goose behavior is evidence about platform semantics, not automatically a local harness defect. Native platform distinction to preserve: summon::delegate creates bounded SessionType::SubAgent executions with isolated conversation/context, source/instruction-only prompt construction, optional filtered inherited extensions, parent-bounded working_dir, max-turn limits, no nested delegation, sync/async modes, max 5 background tasks by default, peek/wait/cancel, 5-second forced abort after cancellation, and completed-result TTL. In contrast platform_extensions::orchestrator manages durable SessionType::User sessions via list/view/start/send_message/interrupt; sessions inherit provider/model and parent extensions and can be revisited. Do not conflate transient delegates, loaded in-session instructions, subrecipes, or persistent native agent sessions. Writer-safety finding to test against local contracts: native delegate guidance says parallel read-only research is safe and parallel writes require strict file partitioning, but delegates default to the parent working directory; working_dir containment prevents escape, not same-file conflicts. Treat file ownership as an orchestrator-level invariant unless local mechanisms enforce stronger isolation. Primary code anchors: crates/goose/src/agents/platform_extensions/summon.rs:51-85,315-413,432-468,508-619,873-1082,1197-1306,1525-1582,1684-1863,1866-1990,2015-2118; crates/goose/src/agents/subagent_handler.rs:26-60,120-268; crates/goose/src/agents/subagent_task_config.rs:8-45; crates/goose/src/prompts/subagent_system.md:1-38; crates/goose/src/agents/platform_extensions/orchestrator.rs:60-105,144-230,234-356,377-558,564-630. Documentation is corroborative only: documentation/docs/guides/context-engineering/subagents.mdx:10-82,265-358 and documentation/docs/tutorials/subagents.md:134-185. Baseline deliverable must record the pin and source-role decision, and define code-over-doc precedence for claims about Goose runtime behavior.
BASELINE LEDGER (2026-07-21): local harness /home/jmercier/Codes/agentic-devlopment at HEAD a169f844ff18459c204a75c3beccc9beb8887890, with the current dirty working corpus explicitly frozen by path+content manifest fingerprint 208:702b361f46f7b6f56db8e116d4060706bb2017b8b33add05188fd684e4574020. Current count definitions explain the older 34/52 expectation: 41 total .specs files, 35 Markdown files under .specs, 13 feature spec.md files (11 tracked at HEAD plus two current untracked feature specs), and 53 docs files (all 53 tracked at HEAD). Entity inventory: 17 skill directories, 13 agent Markdown files, 12 top-level recipe YAML files, plus 10 subrecipe YAML files excluded from the top-level recipe count. Agent-layer acceptance remains provisional until agentic-devlopment-gmma.17.
External pins: GSD /home/jmercier/Codes/third-parties/gsd-core next@6140627f5c5d8a994b1625805efa90b47e82003a; Spec Kit /home/jmercier/Codes/third-parties/spec-kit main@487af97864901462874f18f1c7f8d8adec0b7ddd; Matt Pocock Skills /home/jmercier/Codes/third-parties/mattpocock_skills main@9603c1cc8118d08bc1b3bf34cf714f62178dea3b; Goose /home/jmercier/Codes/third-parties/goose feat/azure-foundry-provider@2de5569b046061994942c16be60d8fcd8da25849.
Authority: active local feature specs and .specs/STATE.md are normative; AGENTS.md is binding operating policy; implemented skills/agents/recipes/scripts/evals are actual-behavior evidence; generated and explanatory docs are subordinate; external frameworks are comparative only unless a local authority adopts them. For Goose runtime claims, pinned source code outranks Goose documentation.
Matrix record schema: concept; layer; artifact/path; canonical local contract citation; local behavior evidence; optional pinned external evidence; expected flow; actual flow; classification; confidence; root cause; existing owner/bead; roster dependency. Finding identity is concept + canonical contract + artifact set + root cause. Classification vocabulary: adopted/equivalent, covered, partial, justified divergence, comparative gap, enhancement candidate, not applicable, local contract violation, contradiction, documentation drift, implementation gap, semantic ambiguity, existing owner. Only local contract violation is binding harness drift.
Known baseline risks: dirty worktree requires use of the fingerprint above; README recipe count is stale (11 versus normative/disk 12); later file mutations must be reported as post-baseline deltas rather than silently absorbed.

title: Audit closure gate: rerun adversarial review and harness judge
description:
Purpose: close the audit loop after child remediation tasks are complete.
Owner role: harness-judge / review-critic.

Description:
Rerun independent adversarial review and harness judge once evidence/preflight/drift/baseline/naming tasks are complete. Update final audit artifacts or create a follow-up audit record with PASS/FAIL and residual risk.

Acceptance criteria:
- review-critic and principal-engineer challenge evidence exists or blocked status is explicitly justified after preflight.
- harness_judge is invoked once after evidence freeze.
- Judge verdict, score, summary, and residual findings are stored under `.audit/harness/`.
- Remediation epic has closure notes linking verification outputs.

Verification:
- `.audit/harness/harness-judge-report.json` contains non-null verdict/score when available.
- Final report or follow-up report records closure status for all findings.
acceptance_criteria: Independent challenge and harness judge run after evidence freeze; residual risks are documented; epic closure evidence is linked.

Owner role: tdd-guide / review-critic.
Requires separate SDD cycle: yes.

Description:
Refine SPEC_DEVIATION marker scanning so instructional examples and fixtures are classified separately from active implementation drift while preserving real drift detection.

Acceptance criteria:
- Current instructional examples no longer block normal scan unless intentionally marked active.
- Active SPEC_DEVIATION markers remain detected and fail/flag verification.
- The marker convention is documented for examples, fixtures, accepted deviations, and active deviations.
- Existing 7 scanner hits are triaged to active/false-positive/accepted/deferred.

Verification:
- `./scripts/find-spec-deviations.sh` exits clean or reports only classified examples in normal mode.
- A fixture with an active marker is still detected.
acceptance_criteria: Scanner separates examples/fixtures from active drift; all existing markers are triaged; active drift remains detectable.

title: F-HIGH-002: Add subagent provider/model preflight and fallback policy
description:
Finding: F-HIGH-002 — Independent adversarial challenge agents unavailable during audit.
Owner role: principal-engineer.
Requires separate SDD cycle: yes.

Description:
Add a provider/model preflight for recipe-required delegated agents and current-session agents. The preflight must detect unavailable deployments and incompatible model options before orchestration attempts delegation.

Acceptance criteria:
- Preflight validates review-critic and principal-engineer delegation configuration before audit/review recipes run.
- Missing deployment and incompatible temperature/thinking errors become explicit preflight blockers with actionable remediation text.
- A safe fallback or operator override path is documented.
- Successful review-critic and principal-engineer challenge outputs can be captured in `.audit/harness/adversarial-review.md`.

Verification:
- Preflight fails fast in a controlled test with a bogus model.
- Preflight passes with the approved available model.
- Rerun challenge produces substantive evidence, not provider errors.
acceptance_criteria: Provider/model issues are caught before delegation; fallback/override is documented; adversarial challenge agents return substantive evidence.

Use injected credential verifier, user repository, session repository, token generator, and clock; do not touch the existing untracked users domain.
acceptance_criteria: AUTH-01: valid credentials issue a session with user id and exact expiry. AUTH-02: unknown users and wrong secrets both return invalid_credentials without issuing a session. AUTH-03: issued sessions resolve to their user before expiry. AUTH-04: expired sessions return session_expired and are revoked. AUTH-05: logout revokes the session and later lookup returns invalid_session.

notes:
## Architecture Audit Ledger: Composable-Skill Architecture × Harness Recipes

**Corpus**: Matt Pocock Skills @ 9603c1cc8118d08bc1b3bf34cf714f62178dea3b
**Harness Version**: Current .goose/recipes/*.yaml (12 recipes)
**Audit Date**: 2026-07-21

---

## 1. CANONICAL EXPECTATION FROM MATT POCOCK SKILLS

### Core Concepts (writing-great-skills/SKILL.md:15-28,42,63; GLOSSARY.md:21-97,131)

| Concept | Definition | Recipe Relevance |
|---------|-----------|------------------|
| **Model-invoked vs User-invoked** | Skills choose invocation axis: model (description kept, context load) vs user (description stripped, cognitive load) | Recipes should not over-own skill methodology; skills decide their own discoverability |
| **Progressive Disclosure** | Move reference down the ladder via context pointers; top stays legible | Recipes should load skills on-demand, not inline methodology |
| **Information Hierarchy** | Steps (primary) → In-skill reference → External reference | Recipe instructions vs delegated skill instructions |
| **Context Load vs Cognitive Load** | Model-invoked costs context; user-invoked costs human memory | Skill loading patterns in recipes |
| **Router Skill** | One user-invoked skill that indexes others | Routing tables in recipes vs skill-level routing |
| **Granularity** | Split by invocation (leading word trigger) or sequence (hide post-completion steps) | Recipe-to-skill composition boundaries |
| **Completion Criterion** | Condition that tells agent work is done | Gates in recipes vs skill-internal criteria |
| **Leading Word** | Compact pretrained concept anchoring behavior | Shared vocabulary across recipes |
| **Prohibition on User-Invoked Chaining** | User-invoked skills cannot fire other user-invoked skills | Recipes cannot chain skills directly |

### Workflow Concepts (grilling/SKILL.md:3; handoff/SKILL.md:8,12)

| Concept | Evidence |
|---------|----------|
| **Alignment Grilling** | Interview relentlessly before acting; resolve dependencies one-by-one |
| **Handoff Discipline** | Compact to handoff doc; suggest skills; do not duplicate captured artifacts |

---

## 2. RECIPE-BY-RECIPE MAPPING

### 2.1 dev.yaml (Orchestration Recipe)

**Classification**: PARTIAL — comparative gap with enhancement candidates

| Aspect | Canonical Expectation | Actual Evidence | Classification | Confidence |
|--------|----------------------|-----------------|----------------|------------|
| Skill Loading Pattern | Progressive disclosure: load only when branch fires | Explicit routing table loads skills per phase: `load skills agentic-devlopment`, `load skills goose-orchestration` etc. | **PARTIAL** — explicit load is local convention, not skill-initiated | HIGH |
| Orchestration vs Methodology | Recipe owns phase order; skill owns methodology | AD-001: "Recipes are hardcoded workflow controllers. Agents are autonomous specialists." | **ADOPTED** — clear boundary | HIGH |
| User-vs-Model Invocation | Skills control their invocation axis | Routing table dictates: "Load skill X → Agent Y" | **COMPARATIVE-GAP** — recipe overrides skill discoverability | MEDIUM |
| Router Pattern | Router skill indexes user-invoked skills | Recipe has inline routing table (not a skill) | **EQUIVALENT** — local convention serves same purpose | HIGH |
| Completion Criteria | Skill-level checkable criteria | Recipe gates: Explore gate, Intent gate, Spec gate, etc. | **ENHANCEMENT-CANDIDATE** — recipe gates supplement skill criteria | MEDIUM |
| Handoff | Handoff doc, suggest skills | Memory checkpoint, `bd remember`, close beads | **PARTIAL** — Beads replaces handoff doc; no "suggest skills" | HIGH |
| Grilling/Alignment | Grill before specification | "Explore gate: source-code evidence exists" before Intent | **COMPARATIVE-GAP** — no grilling step explicit | MEDIUM |

**Root Cause**: dev.yaml is an orchestration recipe (AD-001) that owns workflow, not methodology. Skill loading is explicit to prevent runtime discovery variance.

---

### 2.2 design.yaml (Specialist Recipe)

**Classification**: ADOPTED — equivalent with local adaptation

| Aspect | Canonical Expectation | Actual Evidence | Classification | Confidence |
|--------|----------------------|-----------------|----------------|------------|
| Skill Loading Pattern | Load when branch fires | Phase 1: UX skills; Phase 2: UI skills; sequential not simultaneous | **ADOPTED** — progressive disclosure via phases | HIGH |
| Orchestration vs Methodology | Clear boundary | "AD-001 Pattern: Specialist (sequential dual-phase)" | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Invocation Control | Skills decide discoverability | Recipe controls: `load skills ux-quality`, `load skills ui-quality` | **EQUIVALENT** — local explicit loading | HIGH |
| Stop Behavior | Explicit completion criteria | "Stop/escalation: if Phase 1 produces no findings...stop before Phase 2" | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |

**Root Cause**: Specialist pattern with sequential phases matches progressive disclosure model.

---

### 2.3 doc-review.yaml (Specialist Recipe)

**Classification**: ADOPTED — equivalent

| Aspect | Canonical Expectation | Actual Evidence | Classification | Confidence |
|--------|----------------------|-----------------|----------------|------------|
| Skill Loading Pattern | Load on-demand | `load skills agentic-devlopment`, `load skills beads` before inspection | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Read-Only Discipline | Do not act until confirmed | "This is a read-only review; you MUST NOT modify files" | **ADOPTED** — stricter than Matt: explicit mutation prohibition | HIGH |
| Verdict Semantics | Completion criterion | APPROVE/PASS-WITH-NITS/BLOCK with evidence | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Memory Checkpoint | Handoff discipline | "Do not store memory during this read-only workflow" | **PARTIAL** — read-only specific | HIGH |

**Root Cause**: Review methodology matches. Read-only constraint is local harness policy.

---

### 2.4 explore.yaml (Specialist Recipe)

**Classification**: ADOPTED — equivalent

| Aspect | Canonical Expectation | Actual Evidence | Classification | Confidence |
|--------|----------------------|-----------------|----------------|------------|
| Skill Loading | Load skill before work | `load skills agentic-devlopment first, then run the explore subrecipe` | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Read-Only | Research without acting | "Read-only investigation of code, docs, Beads state" | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Completion Gate | Checkable criteria | 5-point checklist: question answered, files identified, risks assessed, NO mods, NO beads | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Memory Checkpoint | Handoff compact | `bd remember` for durable facts | **PARTIAL** — Beads-backed, not handoff doc | HIGH |

**Root Cause**: Explore matches research/wayfinder concept from Matt corpus.

---

### 2.5 harness-audit.yaml (Orchestration Recipe)

**Classification**: ADOPTED — comprehensive alignment

| Aspect | Canonical Expectation | Actual Evidence | Classification | Confidence |
|--------|----------------------|-----------------|----------------|------------|
| Skill Loading | Progressive disclosure | Load: goose-orchestration, sdd, knowledge-graph, harness-judge; separate phases | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Orchestration Model | Recipe owns flow | "The main session owns orchestration, synthesis, findings..." | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Safety Boundary | Do not act until confirmed | "Do not modify tracked repository files..." 10 phase gates | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Judge Isolation | Split by sequence (hide post-completion steps) | "Keep harness_judge isolated from authoring. Invoke it once, only after evidence is frozen." | **ADOPTED** — matches "split by sequence" | HIGH |

**Root Cause**: harness-audit.yaml most closely matches Matt Pocock architecture principles.

---

### 2.6 harness-review.yaml (Specialist Recipe)

**Classification**: ADOPTED — equivalent

| Aspect | Canonical Expectation | Actual Evidence | Classification | Confidence |
|--------|----------------------|-----------------|----------------|------------|

| Composition | Subrecipe composition | Uses both `review` and `doc-review` subrecipes | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Synthesis | Single source of truth | "Merge findings...Deduplicate overlapping findings" | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Verdict | Completion criterion | APPROVE/PASS-WITH-NITS/BLOCK with self-validation checklist | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |

---

**Classification**: ADOPTED — with local TDD extension

|--------|----------------------|-----------------|----------------|------------|

| Dual-Phase Pattern | Split by sequence | RED phase (tdd-guide) → GREEN phase (implementation-worker) | **ADOPTED** — matches sequence split | HIGH |
| Stop Behavior | Completion criterion | "Max 3 fix iterations before escalating" | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Handoff | Compact with suggested skills | "Recommended next: /review" | **PARTIAL** — route suggestion not skill suggestion | MEDIUM |

**Root Cause**: TDD discipline (RED→GREEN→REFACTOR) is local harness extension of implement.md concept.

---

### 2.8 plan.yaml (Specialist Recipe)

**Classification**: ADOPTED — equivalent with Beads integration

| Aspect | Canonical Expectation | Actual Evidence | Classification | Confidence |
|--------|----------------------|-----------------|----------------|------------|
| Skill Loading | Load before work | `load skills beads`, `load skills sdd` | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Orchestration | Recipe owns flow, skill owns method | Architect decomposes; planner validates graph | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Requirement Traceability | Single source of truth | "Assign [FEAT]-01, [FEAT]-02 IDs. Every story maps to Beads tasks." | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Completion Gate | Checkable | 5-point validation: graph valid, assignee set, acceptance linking, etc. | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |

**Root Cause**: to-tickets.md concept implemented via Beads integration.

---

### 2.9 release.yaml (Specialist Recipe)

**Classification**: PARTIAL — missing alignment grilling

| Aspect | Canonical Expectation | Actual Evidence | Classification | Confidence |
|--------|----------------------|-----------------|----------------|------------|
| Skill Loading | On-demand | `load skills agentic-devlopment first` | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Gate Ordering | Explicit dependency | "Release REQUIRES prior verify PASS" | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Stop Behavior | Stop on first failure | "Stop on first gate FAIL — do not proceed past a failed gate." | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Grilling/Alignment | Grill before acting | No grilling step before release | **COMPARATIVE-GAP** | MEDIUM |

---

### 2.10 review.yaml (Specialist Recipe)

**Classification**: ADOPTED — equivalent

| Aspect | Canonical Expectation | Actual Evidence | Classification | Confidence |
|--------|----------------------|-----------------|----------------|------------|
| Skill Loading | Load first | `load skills code-review first — it detects the review type` | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Adaptive Routing | Branch handling | "review type detected (PR / feature / security / global / release / hotfix)" | **ADOPTED** — matches branch concept | HIGH |
| Gate Stamp | Completion signal | `bd update <bead-id> --add-label env:reviewed` | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Gap Detection | Exhaustive completion | "call remember_memory" for AC-without-test | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |

### 2.11 spec.yaml (Specialist Recipe)

| Completion Criterion | Checkable, exhaustive | "Spec-Anchored Outcome Rule": WHEN/THEN/SHALL format; checkable as failing test | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Closure Gate | 3-check validation | Unambiguity, open questions resolved, implicit dimensions | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Progressive Disclosure | Subrecipe for larger work | `spec-worker subrecipe for isolated writing` | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |

---

### 2.12 verify.yaml (Specialist Recipe)

**Classification**: ADOPTED — with adaptive type dispatch

| Aspect | Canonical Expectation | Actual Evidence | Classification | Confidence |
|--------|----------------------|-----------------|----------------|------------|
| Skill Loading | Load per type | api→agentic-devlopment; web→webapp-testing+ui-quality; etc. | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Adaptive Branching | Branch handling | Type detection: api/web/cli/library/ui/mixed | **ADOPTED** — matches branch concept | HIGH |
| Gate Ordering | Explicit dependency | "Verify REQUIRES a prior review APPROVE" | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Loop Behavior | Max retries | "Max 3 cycles before escalating" | **ADOPTED** | HIGH |
| Spec Coverage | Exhaustive completion | "SPEC-COVERAGE-GAP findings BEFORE running the test suite" | **ENHANCEMENT-CANDIDATE** — local extension | HIGH |

**Root Cause**: QA/verification concept with local spec-traceability extension.

---

## 3. CROSS-CUTTING FINDINGS

### 3.1 Orchestration vs Methodology Ownership

| Recipe | Pattern | Boundary Clarity | Evidence |
|--------|---------|------------------|----------|
| dev.yaml | Orchestration | **CLEAR** | AD-001 stated explicitly |
| harness-audit.yaml | Orchestration | **CLEAR** | 10-phase gated workflow |
| design.yaml | Specialist | **CLEAR** | Sequential dual-phase |
| All others | Specialist | **CLEAR** | Single in-session agent load |

**Classification**: **ADOPTED** — AD-001 explicitly states "Recipes are hardcoded workflow controllers. Agents are autonomous specialists."

### 3.2 User-Visible Routing

| Recipe | Routing Mechanism | Matt Equivalent | Classification |
|--------|-------------------|-----------------|----------------|
| dev.yaml | Inline routing table | Router skill | **EQUIVALENT** |
| verify.yaml | Type detection table | Branch handling | **ADOPTED** |
| review.yaml | Type detection | Branch handling | **ADOPTED** |

**Root Cause**: Routing tables in recipes serve same purpose as router skills. Local convention is explicit tables over skill-level routers.

### 3.3 Prohibition on User-Invoked Chaining

**Matt Expectation** (GLOSSARY.md:21-40): User-invoked skills cannot fire other user-invoked skills.

**Harness Evidence**: Recipes use `load skills` command (Goose platform behavior) which is recipe-controlled, not skill-to-skill invocation. Skills do not load other skills directly.

**Classification**: **NOT-APPLICABLE** — Goose `load skills` is session-level, not skill-level chaining.

### 3.4 Setup Dependencies

**Matt Expectation** (setup-matt-pocock-skills concept): Hard/soft dependency declaration.

**Harness Evidence**: Recipes declare extensions (Beads, Playwright, etc.) and skills explicitly. Dependency ordering via Beads `--deps` flags.

**Classification**: **EQUIVALENT** — Different mechanism (extensions + Beads deps) serves same purpose.

### 3.5 Alignment/Spec/Ticket/Implement/Review Flow

**Matt Expectation**: grilling → to-spec → to-tickets → implement → code-review

**Harness Evidence**: explore → spec → plan → implement → review → verify → release

**Classification**: **EQUIVALENT** — Local harness adds verify/release phases; grilling is implicit in explore.

### 3.6 Stop Behavior

| Recipe | Stop Trigger | Evidence |
|--------|--------------|----------|
| dev.yaml | Escalation after 2 BLOCKs, 3 loops | "after 2 BLOCKs, 3 implement/verify loops...delegate principal-engineer" |
| implement.yaml | Max 3 fix iterations | "Max 3 fix iterations before escalating" |
| verify.yaml | Max 3 cycles | "Max 3 cycles before escalating" |
| design.yaml | Phase 1 blocker | "stop before Phase 2 and report the Phase 1 blocker" |

**Classification**: **ADOPTED** — All recipes have explicit stop conditions.

### 3.7 Recipes Over-Owning Methodology

| Recipe | Methodology Inline? | Evidence | Classification |
|--------|---------------------|----------|----------------|
| dev.yaml | PARTIAL | SDD workflow phases inline | **PARTIAL** — workflow order is recipe concern; SDD detail should be in sdd skill |
| spec.yaml | YES | Spec-Anchored Outcome Rule inline | **PARTIAL** — rule could be in sdd skill |
| plan.yaml | YES | SDD phase decomposition inline | **PARTIAL** — decomposition rules could be in sdd skill |
| implement.yaml | MINIMAL | TDD phases named only | **ADOPTED** — delegated to tdd-guide |
| verify.yaml | MINIMAL | Type detection only | **ADOPTED** — delegated to qa-automation |

**Root Cause**: Orchestration recipes (dev.yaml) have more inline methodology than specialist recipes. This is intentional per AD-001 but creates some methodology duplication.

---

## 4. SUMMARY MATRIX

| Recipe | Classification | Gaps | Enhancements | Root Cause |
|--------|----------------|------|--------------|------------|
| dev.yaml | **PARTIAL** | Grilling not explicit; routing overrides skill discoverability | Phase gates; explicit routing table | Orchestration pattern requires explicit control |
| design.yaml | **ADOPTED** | — | Sequential dual-phase | Matches progressive disclosure |
| doc-review.yaml | **ADOPTED** | — | Read-only constraint | Local harness policy |
| explore.yaml | **ADOPTED** | — | 5-point completion gate | Matches research concept |
| harness-audit.yaml | **ADOPTED** | — | 10-phase gates; judge isolation | Comprehensive alignment |
| harness-review.yaml | **ADOPTED** | — | Subrecipe composition | Matches synthesis concept |
| implement.yaml | **ADOPTED** | — | TDD RED→GREEN→REFACTOR | Local SDD extension |
| plan.yaml | **ADOPTED** | — | Beads traceability | to-tickets equivalent |
| release.yaml | **PARTIAL** | No grilling before release | Gate ordering | Missing alignment step |
| review.yaml | **ADOPTED** | — | Gap detection; gate stamp | code-review equivalent |
| spec.yaml | **ADOPTED** | — | WHEN/THEN/SHALL format | to-spec equivalent |
| verify.yaml | **ADOPTED** | — | Spec coverage gate | QA equivalent with extension |

---

## 5. GOOSE RUNTIME EVIDENCE (from bvuj.1)

**Relevant Goose Behavior** (summon.rs:242-413,547-619):
- `load` composes instructions into current session
- `delegate` creates bounded isolated execution
- Discovery spans agents/recipes/subrecipes

**Harness Alignment**:
- `load skills` and `load agent` match Goose session composition
- Subrecipes match Goose bounded execution
- AD-001 patterns (Orchestration vs Specialist) align with Goose capabilities

**Classification**: **ADOPTED** — Harness recipes use Goose primitives correctly per AD-001.

---

## 6. AUDIT VERDICT

| Category | Count |
|----------|-------|
| **ADOPTED** | 10/12 |
| **PARTIAL** | 2/12 (dev.yaml, release.yaml) |
| **COMPARATIVE-GAP** | 2 (grilling not explicit, routing overrides) |
| **ENHANCEMENT-CANDIDATE** | 3 (phase gates, spec coverage, traceability) |
| **CONTRADICTORY** | 0 |
| **NOT-APPLICABLE** | 1 (user-invoked chaining) |

**Overall Assessment**: Harness recipes demonstrate **high conformance** to Matt Pocock composable-skill architecture. The AD-001 decision record provides clear orchestration-vs-methodology boundary. Gaps are primarily:
1. Explicit grilling/alignment steps before major decisions (dev.yaml, release.yaml)
2. Routing tables in recipes override skill-level discoverability (local convention)

These are **comparative gaps**, not violations — they represent local harness decisions that differ from external corpus patterns but do not contradict local contracts.
close_reason: Architecture audit completed. Evidence ledger recorded with full recipe-by-recipe mapping: 10/12 ADOPTED, 2/12 PARTIAL (dev.yaml, release.yaml), 0 CONTRADICTORY. Key findings: (1) AD-001 provides clear orchestration-vs-methodology boundary matching Matt Pocock principles; (2) Comparative gaps are grilling not explicit in dev/release and routing tables overriding skill discoverability — both are local convention choices, not violations; (3) Goose runtime primitives used correctly per AD-001 patterns. No remediation needed — external difference alone is not drift.

## AGGREGATE FINDINGS

### Cross-Cutting Classifications

| Concept | Local Harness Status | Evidence Basis |
|---------|---------------------|----------------|
| Invocation ownership (user/model boundary) | **comparative-gap** | Local has no disable-model-invocation mechanism; all 17 skills are implicitly model-invoked |
| Router reachability | **covered** | goose-orchestration serves as router skill; beads serves as canonical reference |
| Context/cognitive load tradeoffs | **partial** | Progressive disclosure implemented but no formal context-load measurement |
| Dependency expression | **covered** | "load skill:" prose pattern matches Matt's /skill convention |
| Composability | **covered** | Skills invoke each other via prose; methodology isolation achieved |
| Lifecycle state | **not-applicable** | Local has no lifecycle buckets (no deprecated/, in-progress/, etc.) |
| Docs/eval synchronization | **comparative-gap** | Local has eval JSON files but no plugin.json manifest sync |
| Methodology overlap | **partial** | Some overlap between ui-quality/ux-quality and cognitive-ux/agentic-ux |

### Goose Platform Evidence

| Goose Behavior | Local Claim | Goose Source | Classification |
|----------------|-------------|--------------|----------------|
| Discovery spans agents/recipes/subrecipes | goose-orchestration documents load() | summon.rs:242-413 | Equivalent |
| load() composes into current session | documented in goose-orchestration | summon.rs:547-619 | Equivalent |
| delegate() creates isolated execution | documented in goose-orchestration | summon.rs:547-619 | Equivalent |
| Skill discovery via .agents/skills/ | harness uses this path | convention.rs (implicit) | Equivalent |

### Enhancement Candidates

1. **Invocation axis declaration** — Add disable-model-invocation support to distinguish user-only skills (e.g., harness-judge audit skill should not auto-invoke)
2. **Context load measurement** — Formal metric for skill description token count and progressive disclosure ratio
3. **Lifecycle buckets** — in-progress/, deprecated/ directories for skill maturation
4. **Plugin manifest sync** — Automated sync between skills and any plugin registry

### Comparative Gaps (not violations)

1. Matt uses disable-model-invocation; local has no equivalent
2. Matt has CONTEXT.md for domain language; local uses .specs/STATE.md for ADRs
3. Matt has explicit lifecycle folders; local has implicit "all promoted" model
4. Matt has plugin.json manifest; local has eval JSON files instead

### No Contradictions Found

No local skill contract contradicts Matt's composable-skill architecture. Differences are comparative gaps or enhancement candidates.

---

ARCHITECTURE AUDIT — COMPOSABLE SKILL MAPPING ACROSS 13 HARNESS AGENTS (PRE-ROSTER GATE)

**Audit timestamp**: 2026-07-21 12:24 UTC
**Gate status**: Proceeding against current agent roster (13 agents) BEFORE roster overhaul gate (gmma.17) closes
**Comparative source**: Matt Pocock Skills @ 9603c1cc8118d08bc1b3bf34cf714f62178dea3b
**Harness agent path**: .agents/agents/*.md

CONCEPT CONTRACT REFERENCE (from parent bvuj.17)

Key composable-skill concepts:
• Bounded composability/ownership — skills vs personas, methodology vs identity
• User-vs-model invocation — who triggers what, explicit boundaries
• Truthful routing/triggers — description accuracy, branch coverage
• Progressive disclosure — context pointers, demand-loaded reference
• Hard/soft dependencies — Required Skill Load, conditional loads
• Alignment grilling — pre-spec validation
• Domain language/ADRs — leading words, shared vocabulary
• Conversation→spec→ticket→implementation flow
• TDD/diagnosis/research/review/handoff patterns
• Lifecycle buckets — promoted/deprecated/in-progress
• Explicit non-goals — what skills must not own

notes:

Use Goose runtime to test composability claims: source discovery spans agents/recipes/subrecipes; load composes instructions into the current session, delegate adapts a source into an isolated executable recipe, explicit extension filtering controls capabilities, and source resolution/name precedence can affect routing truthfulness. Goose comparative source (external, non-normative): /home/jmercier/Codes/third-parties/goose; origin https://github.com/aaif-goose/goose.git; inspected local branch feat/azure-foundry-provider at exact revision 2de5569b046061994942c16be60d8fcd8da25849 (2026-07-03). Cite exact revision + repository-relative path/line or symbol. Classify differences using the audit authority model; Goose behavior is evidence about platform semantics, not automatically a local harness defect.
CONCEPT CONTRACT — Composable skills. Pins: Matt Pocock Skills 9603c1cc8118d08bc1b3bf34cf714f62178dea3b; Goose 2de5569.... Comparative categories: bounded composability/ownership; user-vs-model invocation; truthful routing/triggers; model context vs human cognitive load; progressive disclosure/pointers; hard/soft dependencies; alignment grilling; domain language/ADRs; conversation→spec→ticket→implementation; TDD/diagnosis/research/review/handoff; lifecycle buckets; docs/plugin/inventory sync; explicit non-goals. Invocation axes, dependency semantics, lifecycle buckets, two-axis quality loops are comparative gaps/enhancements, not violations. Goose summon.rs:242-413,547-619: discovery spans agents/recipes/subrecipes; load composes current context, delegate creates bounded isolated execution. Flow: truthful discovery→bounded skill→in-session load→context+pointers→dependencies/alignment→spec/ticket/implementation→appropriate quality loops→handoff→inventory sync. ACTIONABLE QUESTIONS: Who may invoke each skill—user, model, agent, or another skill—and is any human-only boundary explicit? Do names/descriptions truthfully represent discoverability and trigger branches? Which dependencies are hard, soft, ordered, cyclic, missing, or failure-producing? Is common material inline and conditional detail behind precise pointers? What alignment/grilling occurs before specification? Which artifact owns domain language and ADR context? Is conversation→spec→ticket→implementation traceable? When are TDD, diagnosis, research, review, and handoff selected? Which lifecycle states exist for draft/promoted/deprecated/retired skills? What automated check synchronizes plugin/docs/inventory? What must each skill explicitly not own?

notes:
## Knowledge-graph Concept × Recipes Evidence Ledger

**Audit date:** 2026-07-21
**Concept:** Knowledge-graph lifecycle and executable conformance
**Canonical expectation sources:**
- `.specs/features/kg-integration/spec.md` (KG-01..KG-06, AC-KG-01..AC-KG-02)
- `AGENTS.md` Change impact rules: "run `node apps/kg/dist/cli.js pipeline` after every structural change"
- Parent ticket bvuj.12 concept contract: "Expected flow: harness/product inputs→bootstrap[--product]→asserted .knowledge/memory.jsonl→reason→derived.jsonl→validate/snapshot→visualize→closure including executable test VALIDATES acceptance_criterion"

**Classification key:**
- **covered** — recipe explicitly invokes or references KG pipeline/tools
- **partial** — recipe has related memory/checkpoint concepts but no KG invocation
- **missing** — recipe would benefit from KG integration per spec but lacks it
- **not-applicable** — recipe scope does not touch structural changes requiring KG
- **contradictory** — recipe contradicts KG spec requirements

---

### Recipe 1: dev.yaml
**Classification:** PARTIAL
**Canonical expectation:** KG-04 "WHEN implement/spec/review/discover subrecipes complete THEN KG entities are created via MCP create_entities"; AGENTS.md "Memory gate: durable learnings are recorded with bd remember, and structural harness knowledge has KG evidence."
**Citation:** dev.yaml lines 43, 265, 282, 308
**Actual evidence:**
- Line 43: "Memory -> record durable learnings with bd remember or knowledge-graph evidence when structural"
- Line 265: "Memory gate: durable learnings are recorded with bd remember, and structural harness knowledge has KG evidence."
- Line 282: review routing includes "knowledge-graph" skill for review-critic
- Line 308: "Store durable facts via bd remember; for structural harness changes, update the knowledge graph pipeline evidence."
**Expected vs actual:** dev.yaml instructs "update the knowledge graph pipeline evidence" textually but does NOT explicitly invoke `node apps/kg/dist/cli.js pipeline` as a gated step. KG skill is loaded for review-critic but no mandatory KG checkpoint per subrecipe completion.
**Drift/gap type:** COMPARATIVE GAP (partial implementation)
**Confidence:** 0.85
**Root cause:** Memory checkpoint pattern exists; KG-04 subrecipe checkpoint requirement is not automated into recipe gates.

---

### Recipe 2: implement.yaml
**Classification:** MISSING
**Canonical expectation:** KG-04 "WHEN implement/spec/review/discover subrecipes complete THEN KG entities are created via MCP create_entities"
**Citation:** implement.yaml full text
**Actual evidence:** No grep matches for 'kg', 'knowledge', 'pipeline', 'entity', 'memory.jsonl'. Only `remember_memory sdd/phase` for phase state persistence (line 110-112).
**Expected vs actual:** Spec KG-04 requires KG entities on implement completion. Recipe has no KG invocation or skill loading.
**Drift/gap type:** MISSING COVERAGE
**Confidence:** 0.95
**Root cause:** implement.yaml designed before KG integration spec; memory checkpoint uses bd remember, not KG pipeline.

---

### Recipe 3: review.yaml
**Classification:** PARTIAL
**Canonical expectation:** KG-05 "WHEN review-critic runs THEN it queries KG for ACs without VALIDATES test AND reports gaps as MEDIUM findings"
**Citation:** review.yaml lines 42-44
**Actual evidence:**
- Lines 42-44: Gap detection uses `remember_memory sdd/gaps "AC-without-test: <AC-ID> | Feature: <feature> | Bead: <bead-id>"` — stores in Beads memory, not KG.
- No `load skills knowledge-graph` in the recipe prompt.
- No `search_nodes` or KG query for AC-without-test gaps.
**Expected vs actual:** KG-05 requires querying KG for ACs without VALIDATES test relations. review.yaml detects gaps but stores them in Beads memory, not KG. No KG skill loaded; no KG query executed.
**Drift/gap type:** COMPARATIVE GAP (different mechanism, same intent)
**Confidence:** 0.90
**Root cause:** Gap detection pattern exists but uses bd remember instead of KG VALIDATES relation queries.

---

### Recipe 4: spec.yaml
**Classification:** MISSING
**Canonical expectation:** KG-04 "WHEN implement/spec/review/discover subrecipes complete THEN KG entities are created via MCP create_entities"; AGENTS.md "After writing or updating a spec: node apps/kg/dist/cli.js pipeline"
**Citation:** spec.yaml full text; .specs/features/.agents/AGENTS.md (subdirectory hints)
**Actual evidence:** No KG references in spec.yaml. Memory checkpoint is `bd remember "Spec for [feature]: canonical source is .specs/features/[feature]/spec.md" --key spec-[feature]-pointer`.
**Expected vs actual:** KG spec and AGENTS.md both require `node apps/kg/dist/cli.js pipeline` after spec changes. Recipe writes spec.md but does not invoke KG pipeline or create AC entities.
**Drift/gap type:** MISSING COVERAGE
**Confidence:** 0.95
**Root cause:** spec.yaml uses Beads memory pointer pattern; KG pipeline invocation not integrated into recipe workflow.

---

### Recipe 5: verify.yaml
**Classification:** MISSING
**Canonical expectation:** AC-KG-02 "KG entities are created via MCP create_entities AND relations (IMPLEMENTED_BY, HAS_CRITERION, etc.) are stored in memory.jsonl"
**Citation:** verify.yaml — "(no KG references)"
**Actual evidence:** Zero matches for any KG-related terms. Recipe focuses on Playwright/test execution and gate stamping (env:verified).
**Expected vs actual:** Verification could record VALIDATES relations to close the AC→Test→Verification loop per KG-05. No such recording occurs.
**Drift/gap type:** MISSING COVERAGE (lower priority — verify is downstream)
**Confidence:** 0.80
**Root cause:** verify.yaml handles test execution gating; KG recording considered out of scope for verification-only workflow.

---

### Recipe 6: explore.yaml
**Classification:** MISSING
**Canonical expectation:** KG-04 "discover subrecipes complete THEN KG entities are created"
**Citation:** explore.yaml full text
**Actual evidence:** Only memory checkpoint via bd remember; no KG references.
**Expected vs actual:** explore.yaml is read-only research. KG-04 mentions "discover" subrecipes — explore.yaml could record research findings as KG evidence.
**Drift/gap type:** COMPARATIVE GAP (ambiguous — read-only may exempt KG writes)
**Confidence:** 0.65

---

### Recipe 7: plan.yaml
**Classification:** MISSING
**Canonical expectation:** AGENTS.md: "run `node apps/kg/dist/cli.js pipeline` after every structural change"; work:beads_task entities per knowledge-graph SKILL.md

### Recipe 8: release.yaml
**Classification:** NOT-APPLICABLE
**Canonical expectation:** KG pipeline invocation required for "structural changes"
**Citation:** release.yaml — no KG references
**Actual evidence:** release.yaml handles deployment gating (env:verified→env:prod). No structural harness changes occur during release.
**Expected vs actual:** release.yaml scope is artifact deployment and gate stamping; no structural changes trigger KG pipeline requirement.
**Drift/gap type:** N/A
**Confidence:** 0.90
**Root cause:** release.yaml is operational, not structural.

---

### Recipe 9: design.yaml
**Classification:** NOT-APPLICABLE
**Canonical expectation:** KG pipeline for "structural changes"
**Citation:** design.yaml — no KG references
**Actual evidence:** design.yaml orchestrates UX research and UI design phases. No structural harness changes.
**Expected vs actual:** design.yaml is product design workflow; KG integration would be for product: namespace entities if design decisions become specs.
**Drift/gap type:** N/A (or ENHANCEMENT CANDIDATE)
**Confidence:** 0.75
**Root cause:** design.yaml focuses on visual/UX quality, not structural SDD artifacts.

---

### Recipe 10: harness-audit.yaml
**Classification:** COVERED
**Canonical expectation:** KG-06 "WHEN visualize runs THEN dist/kg/index.html opens"; full KG lifecycle for audits
**Citation:** harness-audit.yaml lines 93-96, 126-127
**Actual evidence:**
- Line 93-96: "Load skills goose-orchestration, sdd, knowledge-graph, and harness-judge. load skills: knowledge-graph"
- Line 126-127: "Mandatory KG reasoning evidence: run `node apps/kg/dist/cli.js pipeline --output-dir {{ output_dir }}/kg`, run `node apps/kg/dist/cli.js reason --rules`, read `.knowledge/memory.jsonl` and `{{ output_dir }}/kg/derived.jsonl`, and incorporate derived `HAS_STATUS` facts and inferred relations..."
- Line 127: Gate — "If non-mutating KG output is absent or stale after an available KG pipeline, Domain G cannot PASS; do not dirty `.knowledge`."
**Expected vs actual:** harness-audit.yaml explicitly requires KG pipeline execution, derived fact reading, and treats KG output as a gating condition.
**Drift/gap type:** NONE (fully covered)
**Confidence:** 0.98
**Root cause:** harness-audit.yaml is the canonical audit workflow; KG integration is a core requirement.

---

### Recipe 11: harness-review.yaml
**Classification:** PARTIAL
**Canonical expectation:** KG skill loaded for structural review
**Citation:** harness-review.yaml — no explicit KG references
**Actual evidence:** Recipe loads code-review, agentic-devlopment, beads skills but NOT knowledge-graph. References memory checkpoint via bd remember.
**Expected vs actual:** harness-review.yaml reviews harness quality but does not load knowledge-graph skill for structural integrity checks.
**Drift/gap type:** ENHANCEMENT CANDIDATE
**Confidence:** 0.70
**Root cause:** harness-review.yaml focuses on code and doc consistency; KG queries for harness entity integrity not integrated.

---

### Recipe 12: doc-review.yaml
**Classification:** NOT-APPLICABLE
**Canonical expectation:** KG pipeline for structural changes
**Citation:** doc-review.yaml — no KG references
**Actual evidence:** doc-review.yaml is read-only documentation review. No structural changes.
**Expected vs actual:** Read-only review does not trigger KG pipeline requirement.
**Drift/gap type:** N/A
**Confidence:** 0.90
**Root cause:** doc-review.yaml is read-only by design.

---

## Summary Matrix

| Recipe | Classification | KG Invocation | KG Skill Load | Evidence |
|--------|---------------|---------------|---------------|----------|
| dev.yaml | PARTIAL | NO (textual guidance only) | YES (for review-critic) | Lines 43, 265, 282, 308 |
| implement.yaml | MISSING | NO | NO | No KG references |
| review.yaml | PARTIAL | NO | NO | Gap detection via bd remember, not KG query |
| spec.yaml | MISSING | NO | NO | Memory pointer only |
| verify.yaml | MISSING | NO | NO | No KG references |
| explore.yaml | MISSING | NO | NO | Read-only exemption ambiguous |
| plan.yaml | MISSING | NO | NO | Beads graph, not KG |
| release.yaml | NOT-APPLICABLE | NO | NO | Operational, not structural |
| design.yaml | NOT-APPLICABLE | NO | NO | Product design, not SDD structural |
| harness-audit.yaml | COVERED | YES (gated) | YES | Lines 93-96, 126-127 |
| harness-review.yaml | PARTIAL | NO | NO | Code/doc focus, not KG integrity |
| doc-review.yaml | NOT-APPLICABLE | NO | NO | Read-only |

---

## Key Findings

1. **Single full coverage:** Only harness-audit.yaml fully implements KG lifecycle (KG pipeline invocation, derived facts, gating).

2. **KG-04 gap:** Spec requires KG entities on subrecipe completion (implement/spec/review/discover). None of these recipes invoke KG pipeline or create_entities.

3. **KG-05 gap:** review.yaml detects AC-without-test gaps via bd remember, not KG VALIDATES relation queries.

4. **Pattern:** Recipes use Beads memory (bd remember) as the durable state mechanism. KG integration is advisory text, not automated gates.

5. **Read-only exemption:** explore.yaml and doc-review.yaml may be legitimately exempt from KG writes due to read-only safety boundary.

6. **Root cause:** KG-integration spec (KG-04, AC-KG-02) predates or was not retrofitted into individual recipe workflows. Only harness-audit.yaml was designed with KG as a first-class requirement.

Root cause: KG-04/KG-05 spec requirements not retrofitted into individual recipe gates. Full ledger in ticket notes.

**Repeated Symptom Pattern:**
The 3 MISSING agents (planner, qa-automation, tdd-guide) share a pattern: they author artifacts that should link to KG entities but lack VALIDATES relation guidance. KG integration designed around orientation/query (read) rather than provenance recording (write).

**Root Cause Analysis:**
- Primary: KG integration focused on gap detection (KG-05: review-critic) not gap prevention
- Secondary: Beads and KG remain parallel systems
- Tertiary: Test authoring agents delegate KG concerns to skills implicitly

## Research: agentic-devlopment-bvuj.11.1 — Memory across harness skills

Read-only mapping completed for the 17 current skill entities under `.agents/skills/*/SKILL.md`. Local authority defines Beads as the durable control plane for tasks/discoveries/dependencies/gates/memory/handoffs, `bd remember` as short stable-key/pointer memory, `.knowledge/memory.jsonl` as typed structural KG memory, and forbids read-only audit mutation. External Goose/Spec Kit/GSD/Matt Pocock sources were not needed for binding findings; per Beads, they remain comparative unless adopted.

### Tests / evaluation artifacts located

- Skill eval inventory exists under `evals/skills/` per repository structure and generated-table rules in `AGENTS.md`; not executed due read-only/no-build constraint.

- Exploration boundary: evidence read was limited to Beads records, `.agents/skills/*/SKILL.md`, and canonical recipe/skill memory anchors. No external comparative repositories were inspected because binding local expectations were sufficient for classification.

### Risks

- `.agents/skills/wcag-accessibility-audit/SKILL.md:45-193` — standalone audit skill lacks local Beads/memory boundary language; if invoked in harness review-style work, durable accessibility findings may be reported without Beads linkage.
- `.agents/skills/code-review/SKILL.md:177-186` — loads Beads for “prime, ready, claim, close, remember” but local file does not repeat the recipe rule that review findings must not be stored in memory; relies on `.goose/recipes/harness-review.yaml:71-75`.
- `.agents/skills/systematic-debugging/SKILL.md:300-309` — closes fix bead with root cause/regression evidence but has no durable lesson/pointer memory step; reusable debugging discoveries may remain only in bead close text.
- `.agents/skills/ux-quality/SKILL.md:89-99` — has Beads loop but no concrete pointer-memory example, unlike `ui-quality` and `webapp-testing`; domain reusable UX findings may be under-specified.

### Blast radius

- `.agents/skills/beads/SKILL.md` — changing memory syntax or pointer rules affects all skills that defer Beads commands to it: `agentic-devlopment`, `sdd`, `goose-orchestration`, `code-review`, `systematic-debugging`, `ui-quality`, `ux-quality`, `webapp-testing`.
- `.agents/skills/agentic-devlopment/SKILL.md` — changing durable-state rules affects implementation, orchestration, read-only review handoffs, and lesson capture across development sessions.
- `.agents/skills/knowledge-graph/SKILL.md` — changing `.knowledge/memory.jsonl` or contradiction policy affects structural audit/KG flows and any harness ontology work.
- `.goose/recipes/dev.yaml` and `.goose/recipes/harness-review.yaml` — recipe-level memory checkpoint changes would alter expected skill behavior around `bd remember`, follow-up beads, and review-finding retention.

### Proposed follow-up

None executed and none created. Per task instruction, no Beads mutation or remediation creation was performed.

CONCEPT CONTRACT — Memory. Placement: durable tasks/discoveries/dependencies/gates/memory/handoffs belong in Beads, not chat/TODOs (dev.yaml:12-13); actionable review findings become beads, never memory (harness-review.yaml:75); review proposes one durable keyed quality fact or none (68,73,201); read-only audit does not mutate memory/Beads (harness-audit.yaml:76,195); audits read .knowledge/memory.jsonl and derived.jsonl without dirtying .knowledge (126-127). Flow: classify transient/actionable/reusable/decision/spec/docs/KG-derived → retain transient only in session → actionable issue → stable-key reusable fact → read KG state without mutation → explicit freshness/contradiction status. Questions: ADR/spec/docs boundary, stable-key replacement/versioning, freshness validator, retention/deletion/archive, KG-vs-Beads authority, and promotion trigger. These are semantic ambiguities/enhancement candidates, not proven violations. AC: PASS; owner bvuj.11, architect adjudicates placement; no mutation.

### Classification Totals
- **local-contract-covered**: 12 (AD-001 compliance in all recipes)
- **equivalent**: 14 (spec format, traceability, ordering, TDD, etc.)

- **partial/comparative-gap**: 6 (constitution, clarification, convergence, checklist, analyze)
- **missing**: 1 (cross-artifact analysis command)
- **contradictory-to-local-contract**: 0

### Identified Gaps (comparative only, not local contract violations)
1. **Constitution workflow**: No /constitution command; no constitution.md template generation
2. **Clarification phase**: No /clarify command; ambiguity resolution implicit in /spec
3. **Requirements checklist**: No /checklist command for pre-planning spec quality
4. **Cross-artifact analysis**: No /analyze command for spec/plan/tasks consistency
5. **Convergence workflow**: No explicit flow-back-spec mutation workflow post-implementation

### Strengths vs Spec Kit
1. **AD-001 session patterns**: Formal specialist vs orchestration distinction
2. **Cross-recipe gates**: env:dev→reviewed→verified→prod label state machine
3. **TDD enforcement**: Mandatory RED phase before implementation
4. **Beads integration**: Durable task graph with AC linkage, claims, dependencies
5. **Escalation contracts**: Max retry limits with explicit escalation paths
6. **LLM-as-judge**: Independent harness-judge for audit verdicts

# Gates Audit: Harness Recipes (12 top-level)

## Concept Contract Reference (from bvuj.8)
Canonical lifecycle: Explore → Intent → Spec → AC → Bead → RED → Code → Local validation → Review → Gated verification → Memory
Environment state machine: env:dev → env:reviewed → env:verified → env:prod
Key constraint: verification requires reviewed; no self-approval
Gate evidence: review APPROVE/PASS-WITH-NITS → env:reviewed label; verify PASS → env:verified label; release success → env:prod label
Goose summon.rs constraint: delegates run GooseMode::Auto (approval modes hang); Auto success ≠ approval; parent orchestration must enforce gates

## Evidence Sources
- .specs/features/verify-review-gate/spec.md: GATE-01 through GATE-10 AC definitions
- .specs/features/harness-core/spec.md: AC-RECIPE-05 cross-recipe ordering gates
- .specs/features/harness-audit-remediation/spec.md: HAR-01 canonical lifecycle, HAR-05 deterministic gates
- .agents/agents/orchestrator.md:89-90 VERIFY/REVIEW gate evidence

- .agents/agents/planner.md:54,64-72 dependency graph gates

## CROSS-CUTTING FINDINGS

### F1: env state machine fully implemented in core recipes
The 4-state machine (env:dev → env:reviewed → env:verified → env:prod) is fully implemented in review.yaml, verify.yaml, and release.yaml with explicit gate checks and halt-on-missing behavior.

### F2: Goose Auto-mode constraint acknowledged but external
Concept contract notes delegates run GooseMode::Auto (summon.rs:1244-1255,1777-1788). This is platform semantics, not harness defect. Harness correctly enforces gates via Beads labels at parent/orchestrator level.

### F3: dev.yaml is the central gate orchestrator
Implements all 10 SDD gates and delegates to specialists. Aligns with AD-001 Orchestration pattern. No lifecycle inversion when using /dev as entry.

### F4: PARTIAL ratings are appropriate separations
explore.yaml, design.yaml, doc-review.yaml are correctly gate-light:
- explore: pre-implementation research (no artifacts to gate)
- design: pre-implementation (env labels apply to code)
- doc-review: documentation workflow (not in code lifecycle)

### F5: No contradictions found
No recipe contradicts another's gate behavior. All REQUIRES/STAMPS relationships consistent with canonical spec.

## PATTERN ANALYSIS

### Consistently covered:
1. **Maker/Checker separation**: 12/13 agents have explicit Maker/Checker sections (harness-judge and review-critic implicit as checkers)
2. **Self-approval prohibition**: 11/13 have explicit self-approval rules
3. **Phase gates**: 13/13 have phase-based workflow structure

### Systemic gaps:
1. **env: labels**: Only orchestrator (lines 89-90) mentions env:verified and env:reviewed. 12/13 agents have NO environment lifecycle labels.
   - Gap type: MISSING
   - Root cause: Environment label system is orchestrator-level, not agent-level contract
   - Confidence: HIGH

2. **Release authority**: No agent defines release gate conditions or authority
   - Gap type: MISSING
   - Root cause: Release is treated as orchestrator gate exit, not agent responsibility
   - Confidence: HIGH

3. **Rollback gate**: No agent mentions rollback authority or procedure
   - Gap type: MISSING
   - Root cause: Not part of current agent contract model
   - Confidence: HIGH

4. **Memory gate**: Only orchestrator mentions memory persistence (line 92)
   - Gap type: PARTIAL
   - Root cause: Memory is knowledge-graph skill responsibility, not agent intrinsic
   - Confidence: MEDIUM

5. **env:prod gate**: Not explicitly mentioned anywhere; verification stops at env:verified
   - Gap type: MISSING
   - Root cause: Prod promotion may be human-only gate (not documented)
   - Confidence: MEDIUM

## DRIFT ANALYSIS

### No drift detected:
- All observed gate patterns align with local contracts
- Maker/checker model is consistently applied
- Self-approval prohibitions are correctly scoped

### Enhancement candidates (from Spec Kit / GSD comparison):
1. Environment label propagation to specialist agents (currently orchestrator-only)
2. Explicit release authority assignment
3. Rollback procedure and authority definition
4. Memory gate conditions beyond "if durable"

title: Concept audit: Lifecycle, quality, approval, and release gates
description: Audit RED/GREEN, claim, review, verification, release, coverage, security, deviation, and human-approval gates including stop behavior and evidence. Establish canonical expectations from active specs and decisions, distinguish normative from explanatory/historical sources, and define audit questions before mapping.
acceptance_criteria: Authority-ranked source ledger exists; each expectation has path/heading evidence; ambiguities and contradictions are explicit; expected flow and completion conditions are stated; relevant Beads owners are recorded; no implementation mutation occurs.
notes:
Comparative GSD source: /home/jmercier/Codes/third-parties/gsd-core at revision 6140627f5c5d8a994b1625805efa90b47e82003a (next; https://github.com/open-gsd/gsd-core.git). GSD means Git. Ship. Done. Treat GSD as external reference evidence, not as a harness requirement unless an active harness spec explicitly adopts it. Compare equivalent intent, justified divergence, missing coverage, and transferable enhancement patterns.
Spec Kit comparison source: /home/jmercier/Codes/third-parties/spec-kit at revision 487af97864901462874f18f1c7f8d8adec0b7ddd (main; https://github.com/github/spec-kit.git). Treat GitHub Spec Kit as an external SDD reference implementation, not as a harness requirement unless an active local specification explicitly adopts its behavior. Compare equivalent intent, justified divergence, missing coverage, and transferable enhancement patterns.
Matt Pocock Skills comparison source: /home/jmercier/Codes/third-parties/mattpocock_skills at revision 9603c1cc8118d08bc1b3bf34cf714f62178dea3b (main; https://github.com/mattpocock/skills). This corpus is a composable-skill architecture and engineering-practice reference, not an end-to-end framework and not a local requirement unless adopted. Compare invocation topology, routing, context economy, progressive disclosure, alignment, domain language, workflow composition, quality loops, handoffs, lifecycle buckets, and inventory synchronization; classify external differences as equivalence, justified divergence, comparative gap, or enhancement candidate rather than drift.
Delegation: extract calibrated audit criteria and observable evidence requirements. Read-only; do not grade matrix cells before their evidence exists.
Gate-relevant runtime fact: native subagents currently run GooseMode::Auto because approval-requiring modes can hang until ActionRequired forwarding exists (summon.rs:1244-1255,1777-1788). Audit whether harness maker/checker and human gates occur outside delegates and are actually enforceable. Goose comparative source (external, non-normative): /home/jmercier/Codes/third-parties/goose; origin https://github.com/aaif-goose/goose.git; inspected local branch feat/azure-foundry-provider at exact revision 2de5569b046061994942c16be60d8fcd8da25849 (2026-07-03). Cite exact revision + repository-relative path/line or symbol. Classify differences using the audit authority model; Goose behavior is evidence about platform semantics, not automatically a local harness defect.
CONCEPT CONTRACT — Gates. Canonical lifecycle: Explore source+web → Intent → Spec → AC → Bead → RED → Code → Local validation → Review → gated verification → Memory. Environment: env:dev→env:reviewed→env:verified→env:prod; verification requires reviewed; no self-approval. Evidence dimensions: product-owner.md:3,16,28,34; planner.md:3,24,54,64-72,125-126; orchestrator.md:98,158,183-190. Local evidence covers requirements score and milestone gates plus independent review; RED, exact environment persistence, coverage/security/deviation, release authority, rollback, and memory gate require mapping evidence. Goose summon.rs:1244-1255,1777-1788 forces delegates to Auto because approval modes can hang; Auto success is not approval, so durable parent orchestration must enforce gates. Questions identify gate writer, signed evidence, review-vs-verification separation, thresholds, deviations, release/human authority. Treating delegate success as approval would violate local contract if observed. AC: PASS with targeted evidence gaps; owner bvuj.8 and responsible roles product-owner/planner/orchestrator/review-critic; no mutation.
close_reason: Concept contract ledger completed read-only; authority, questions, expected flow, classifications, citations, gaps, and ownership recorded.

title: Map loop-engineering across harness recipes
description: Map the loop-engineering concept against all 12 current top-level recipes at .goose/recipes/*.yaml. Audit composition, ordering, persona pattern, routing, state transitions, gates, and stop behavior. Evidence collection only, not remediation.
acceptance_criteria: Every current entity is listed and marked covered, partial, missing, contradictory, or not-applicable; applicable statuses cite expectation and artifact evidence; expected-versus-actual flow, drift/gap/enhancement class, confidence, existing owner, and candidate root cause are recorded; repeated symptoms are not separate fixes.
notes:
## Loop-Engineering Evidence Ledger — Recipes Layer

### Concept Contract Reference
From bvuj.7 CLOSED:
- Flow: claim bead/assignee → pre-delegation checks → isolated maker evidence → independent review → correction/retry within 2 review BLOCK and 3 implement/verify cycles → inspect sessions before stuck retry → principal-engineer at >=2 BLOCKs → halt/create escalation bead/report user at 3 unresolved iterations → close only on checker success
- Goose summon.rs:873-1082 exposes peek/wait/cancel, 5-minute wait return, 5-second cancel abort, TTL, and no automatic retry
- Questions: counter independence/persistence, restart reconstruction, slow/stuck/failed state taxonomy, retry strategy change, escalation authority, handback payload, review-before-verification ordering

### Canonical Expectation Sources
1. orchestrator.md:98-99,112,118-119,125,135-144,178,183-190,196,218,221,230,237 — loop bounds, escalation thresholds, maker/checker
2. Goose agent.rs:68 DEFAULT_MAX_TURNS=1000, retry.rs RetryManager with attempts counter
3. Recipe max_turns settings (per-recipe bounds)

---

## Evidence Matrix by Recipe

### 1. design.yaml
**Classification**: PARTIAL
**Expectation**: Bounded iteration on UX/UI phases; escalation on browser capture failure
**Citation**: design.yaml:9-12 (stop/escalation comment)
**Evidence**:
- Stop/escalation comment: "if Phase 1 produces no findings...stop before Phase 2 and report"; "If browser evidence cannot be captured after 2 attempts, report findings as code-inspection-only"
- max_turns: 35
- No explicit retry counter or escalation to principal-engineer
**Expected vs Actual**: Expects bounded browser retry (2 attempts); actual implementation is instructional text, not code-enforced gate
**Gap Type**: Enhancement candidate — instructional retry bound without programmatic enforcement
**Confidence**: Medium
**Root Cause**: Browser capture retry is guidance, not a hard gate with counter state

### 2. dev.yaml
**Classification**: COVERED
**Expectation**: Full loop-engineering contract with explicit bounds
**Citation**: dev.yaml:29-31 (escalation thresholds), dev.yaml:32-36 (verification scoring), dev.yaml:41 (max_turns: 40)
**Evidence**:
- "after 2 BLOCKs, 3 implement/verify loops, or verification score medium-or-lower after a fix attempt, delegate principal-engineer"
- "Max 3 loops: escalate to principal-engineer or ask the user only for the missing decision"
- Explicit verification scoring with loop-back on medium/low
- Full routing table with named agents and explicit escalation paths
- Step 5 hardcodes: "Max 3 implement <-> verify cycles per bead. Max 2 review BLOCK cycles per bead. On limit breach: create or update a blocker bead, delegate principal-engineer"
**Expected vs Actual**: Matches concept contract — 2 BLOCK + 3 implement/verify + explicit escalation
**Gap Type**: None — covered
**Confidence**: High
**Root Cause**: N/A

### 3. doc-review.yaml
**Classification**: NOT-APPLICABLE
**Expectation**: Read-only review; no iteration/retry expected
**Citation**: doc-review.yaml:4-5 ("read-only review; you MUST NOT modify files")
**Evidence**:
- Explicitly read-only workflow
- No iteration loops; single-pass review
- max_turns: 35
- Verdict is terminal (APPROVE/PASS-WITH-NITS/BLOCK) with no retry mechanism
**Expected vs Actual**: N/A — read-only workflows have no loop engineering requirement
**Gap Type**: Not applicable
**Confidence**: High
**Root Cause**: Design intent is single-pass read-only

### 4. explore.yaml
**Classification**: NOT-APPLICABLE
**Expectation**: Read-only investigation; no write loops
**Citation**: explore.yaml:3 ("Read-only investigation")
**Evidence**:
- Read-only investigation
- Completion gate: "NO file modifications made (read-only)"
- max_turns: 30
- No retry or iteration expected
**Expected vs Actual**: N/A — read-only
**Gap Type**: Not applicable
**Confidence**: High
**Root Cause**: Read-only by design

### 5. harness-audit.yaml
**Classification**: PARTIAL
**Expectation**: Gated phases with explicit blockers; no remediation retry
**Citation**: harness-audit.yaml:62-74 (phases with hard gates)
**Evidence**:
- 11 phases with hard gates (phase: 0-10 with input/output/gate criteria)
- "Delegation fallback: if any mandatory delegated evidence collector returns empty output...record the delegate as blocked and immediately replace it with deterministic extraction"
- No retry loops — blocker recording instead

**Classification**: PARTIAL
**Expectation**: Review verdict with no write-loop escalation

**Evidence**:

- "For each failing check: bd create... After fix: re-verify that specific check. Max 3 cycles before escalating."
- env:verified stamp on all PASS
- Cross-recipe ordering gate (env:reviewed required before verify)
- Spec AC coverage gate
- max_turns: 40
**Expected vs Actual**: Matches contract — 3 cycle bound with escalation
**Gap Type**: None — covered
**Confidence**: High
**Root Cause**: N/A

## Summary Classification

| Recipe | Classification | Confidence | Key Gap |
|--------|---------------|------------|---------|
| design.yaml | PARTIAL | Medium | Browser retry instructional only |
| dev.yaml | COVERED | High | — |
| doc-review.yaml | NOT-APPLICABLE | High | Read-only |
| explore.yaml | NOT-APPLICABLE | High | Read-only |
| harness-audit.yaml | PARTIAL | Medium | Delegation fallback but no escalation path |
| harness-review.yaml | PARTIAL | High | No escalation on BLOCK (justified) |
| implement.yaml | COVERED | High | — |
| plan.yaml | PARTIAL | Medium | Cycle detection but no auto-resolution |
| release.yaml | COVERED | High | — |
| review.yaml | COVERED | High | — |
| spec.yaml | PARTIAL | Medium | Revision cycle bound instructional |
| verify.yaml | COVERED | High | — |

### Aggregate Statistics
- COVERED: 6 (dev, implement, release, review, verify, harness-review justified)
- PARTIAL: 4 (design, harness-audit, plan, spec)
- NOT-APPLICABLE: 2 (doc-review, explore)
- MISSING: 0
- CONTRADICTORY: 0

### Cross-Cutting Findings

1. **Counter persistence**: No recipe tracks iteration counters across sessions. Goose agent.rs:250 RetryManager exists but recipes rely on instructional bounds. Classification: Enhancement candidate.

2. **Escalation authority**: dev.yaml explicitly delegates to principal-engineer at thresholds. Other recipes (design, plan, spec) mention escalation but lack explicit delegation. Classification: Enhancement candidate.

3. **Review-before-verify ordering**: Implemented via env:reviewed label gate in verify.yaml:60-71. Classification: Covered.

4. **max_turns per recipe**: All 12 recipes have explicit max_turns (30-40). This is a global loop bound. Classification: Covered.

5. **Goose native retry vs harness retry**: Goose RetryManager handles transient LLM failures; harness recipes handle semantic/workflow retries. Classification: Justified divergence.

### Existing Owner
- Loop engineering contract: orchestrator.md, dev.yaml
- Escalation: principal-engineer agent
- Counter infrastructure: Enhancement candidate (no current owner)

### Candidate Root Causes (Repeated Symptoms)
1. Instructional bounds without programmatic enforcement (design, spec) — Root: recipes are LLM-driven, not code-driven
2. Delegation fallback without escalation (harness-audit) — Root: audit is evidence collection, not remediation
3. Revision cycles conceptual, not tracked (spec) — Root: spec is iterative by nature, no counter infrastructure

## CONCEPT CONTRACT SUMMARY (from bvuj.7)

**Canonical expectation:**
- Flow: claim → pre-delegation → maker → checker → correction/retry (≤2 BLOCK, ≤3 impl/verify) → escalation → close on success
- Counter bounds: 2 review BLOCK max, 3 implement/verify cycles max
- Escalation: principal-engineer at ≥2 BLOCKs; halt/bead/user at 3 unresolved
- Close gate: checker success required
- Goose native: no automatic retry; 5-min wait return; 5-sec cancel abort; TTL expiry

**Primary citation:** orchestrator.md:135-144,183-190,218,221,230,237

title: Concept audit: Loop, retry, recovery, and escalation engineering
description: Audit success conditions, bounded iteration, durable counters, retry strategy changes, restart safety, circuit breakers, handback, and named escalation. Establish canonical expectations from active specs and decisions, distinguish normative from explanatory/historical sources, and define audit questions before mapping.
acceptance_criteria: Authority-ranked source ledger exists; each expectation has path/heading evidence; ambiguities and contradictions are explicit; expected flow and completion conditions are stated; relevant Beads owners are recorded; no implementation mutation occurs.
notes:
Comparative GSD source: /home/jmercier/Codes/third-parties/gsd-core at revision 6140627f5c5d8a994b1625805efa90b47e82003a (next; https://github.com/open-gsd/gsd-core.git). GSD means Git. Ship. Done. Treat GSD as external reference evidence, not as a harness requirement unless an active harness spec explicitly adopts it. Compare equivalent intent, justified divergence, missing coverage, and transferable enhancement patterns.
Spec Kit comparison source: /home/jmercier/Codes/third-parties/spec-kit at revision 487af97864901462874f18f1c7f8d8adec0b7ddd (main; https://github.com/github/spec-kit.git). Treat GitHub Spec Kit as an external SDD reference implementation, not as a harness requirement unless an active local specification explicitly adopts its behavior. Compare equivalent intent, justified divergence, missing coverage, and transferable enhancement patterns.
Matt Pocock Skills comparison source: /home/jmercier/Codes/third-parties/mattpocock_skills at revision 9603c1cc8118d08bc1b3bf34cf714f62178dea3b (main; https://github.com/mattpocock/skills). This corpus is a composable-skill architecture and engineering-practice reference, not an end-to-end framework and not a local requirement unless adopted. Compare invocation topology, routing, context economy, progressive disclosure, alignment, domain language, workflow composition, quality loops, handoffs, lifecycle buckets, and inventory synchronization; classify external differences as equivalence, justified divergence, comparative gap, or enhancement candidate rather than drift.
Delegation: extract calibrated audit criteria and observable evidence requirements. Read-only; do not grade matrix cells before their evidence exists.
Required runtime comparison: native background tasks expose peek/wait/cancel and states but no automatic task retry policy; wait returns control after 5 minutes while task continues, cancellation escalates to abort after 5 seconds, and results expire by TTL. Compare these mechanics with harness retry, stuck-session, and escalation promises. Goose comparative source (external, non-normative): /home/jmercier/Codes/third-parties/goose; origin https://github.com/aaif-goose/goose.git; inspected local branch feat/azure-foundry-provider at exact revision 2de5569b046061994942c16be60d8fcd8da25849 (2026-07-03). Cite exact revision + repository-relative path/line or symbol. Classify differences using the audit authority model; Goose behavior is evidence about platform semantics, not automatically a local harness defect.
CONCEPT CONTRACT — Loop/retry/recovery/escalation. Flow: claim bead/assignee → pre-delegation checks → isolated maker evidence → independent review → correction/retry within two review BLOCK and three implement/verify cycles → inspect sessions before stuck retry → principal-engineer at >=2 BLOCKs → halt/create escalation bead/report user at three unresolved iterations → close only on checker success. Evidence: .agents/agents/orchestrator.md:98-99,112,118-119,125,135-144,178,183-190,196,218,221,230,237. Goose summon.rs:873-1082 exposes peek/wait/cancel, 5-minute wait return, 5-second cancel abort, TTL, and no automatic retry. Questions: counter independence/persistence, restart reconstruction, slow/stuck/failed state taxonomy, retry strategy change, escalation authority, handback payload, and review-before-verification ordering. Native-vs-local retry is justified divergence; durable counters/restart/circuit breaker are enhancement candidates; review ordering is semantic ambiguity pending mapping evidence. AC: PASS with owner details partial; owner bvuj.7, escalation role principal-engineer, roster gate gmma.17; no mutation.
close_reason: Concept contract ledger completed read-only; authority, questions, expected flow, classifications, citations, gaps, and ownership recorded.

- Context budgets: lean coordinator, targeted worker context
- Load vs delegate semantics: load injects into current session; delegate creates isolated sub-session

- Context isolation: subagent context construction from source+instructions, not parent transcript

- `.agents/skills/ux-quality/SKILL.md:105,115-118` — role-collision risk: French “skills spécialisés” section and frontend-style phase language blur UX-quality vs frontend-blueprint ownership.

- `.agents/skills/knowledge-graph/SKILL.md:3` — read/write ambiguity: description spans create/update and read-only audit uses; safe only when caller scopes tools, as this session did.
- `.agents/skills/harness-judge/SKILL.md:3` — router scanability gap: single dense description line contains many domains and exclusions, but remains under bloat threshold and has evidence gates in body.

### Blast radius

- `.agents/skills/*/SKILL.md` frontmatter descriptions — changes affect Goose skill discovery/routing and any recipe/agent that relies on “Load when…” matching.
- `.agents/skills/goose-orchestration/SKILL.md` — changes affect delegation contracts, load/delegate semantics, and parallel write-safety guidance across orchestrated sessions.
- `.agents/skills/ux-quality/SKILL.md` + `.agents/skills/frontend-blueprint/SKILL.md` — overlapping workflow language can affect routing between UX audit and frontend implementation/review tasks.
- `.agents/skills/wcag-accessibility-audit/SKILL.md` + UI/UX skill cluster — unnormalized sibling-skill references can affect accessibility-vs-usability delegation decisions.

Delegation audit:
- Workers used: none — direct inspection sufficient
- Scopes assigned: direct read-only inspection of `.agents/skills/*/SKILL.md`, referenced Beads, and comparative pinned sources
- Overlap: none
- Integration: findings synthesized into one evidence ledger with local-vs-comparative authority separated

title: Concept audit: Prompt engineering and context architecture
description: Audit role identity, prompt defense, input/tool contracts, progressive disclosure, output schemas, context budgets, reminders, and instruction conflicts. Establish canonical expectations from active specs and decisions, distinguish normative from explanatory/historical sources, and define audit questions before mapping.
acceptance_criteria: Authority-ranked source ledger exists; each expectation has path/heading evidence; ambiguities and contradictions are explicit; expected flow and completion conditions are stated; relevant Beads owners are recorded; no implementation mutation occurs.
notes:
Comparative GSD source: /home/jmercier/Codes/third-parties/gsd-core at revision 6140627f5c5d8a994b1625805efa90b47e82003a (next; https://github.com/open-gsd/gsd-core.git). GSD means Git. Ship. Done. Treat GSD as external reference evidence, not as a harness requirement unless an active harness spec explicitly adopts it. Compare equivalent intent, justified divergence, missing coverage, and transferable enhancement patterns.
Spec Kit comparison source: /home/jmercier/Codes/third-parties/spec-kit at revision 487af97864901462874f18f1c7f8d8adec0b7ddd (main; https://github.com/github/spec-kit.git). Treat GitHub Spec Kit as an external SDD reference implementation, not as a harness requirement unless an active local specification explicitly adopts its behavior. Compare equivalent intent, justified divergence, missing coverage, and transferable enhancement patterns.
Matt Pocock Skills comparison source: /home/jmercier/Codes/third-parties/mattpocock_skills at revision 9603c1cc8118d08bc1b3bf34cf714f62178dea3b (main; https://github.com/mattpocock/skills). This corpus is a composable-skill architecture and engineering-practice reference, not an end-to-end framework and not a local requirement unless adopted. Compare invocation topology, routing, context economy, progressive disclosure, alignment, domain language, workflow composition, quality loops, handoffs, lifecycle buckets, and inventory synchronization; classify external differences as equivalence, justified divergence, comparative gap, or enhancement candidate rather than drift.
Delegation: extract calibrated audit criteria and observable evidence requirements. Read-only; do not grade matrix cells before their evidence exists.
